surface.name: Mux Video curl path, Stream videos in five minutes
surface.selection_basis: Mux's docs home lists Stream videos in five minutes as its core get-started route. The guide explicitly promises upload and playback in five minutes or less and demonstrates a shareable browser preview.

alternatives_considered.name: Official SDK
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The same guide offers several SDK tabs; curl is a complete request without language package installation.

alternatives_considered.name: Mux live streaming
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a separate starter guide; the docs home places on-demand Stream videos in five minutes first.

entry_point.developer_intent: Submit a sample video to Mux, wait for processing, and watch the streamed result.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived at the official five-minute guide.
No Mux token or asset exists yet.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A publicly playable Mux asset reaches ready state and streams in the browser through its playback ID.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The asset API reports `status: ready`, and opening `https://stream.new/v/{PLAYBACK_ID}` displays the streaming video.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The guide's Step 4 is `Watch your Video` and provides a browser preview URL before asset management and next steps.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: It is the first rendered media result in Mux's official core onboarding guide.

prerequisites.requirement: A Mux account with dashboard access is required to generate a token.
The selected route uses curl and a browser; an online video URL is required, with sample URLs provided.

primary_path.action: Create an access token in the Mux dashboard.
details: Keep the Production environment selected for the guide, grant Mux Video Read and Write, name the token, and click Generate token.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The secret must be saved securely.

primary_path.action: POST a public video URL to the Mux assets endpoint using Basic authentication.
details: Use the documented sample URL if needed, public playback policy, and basic video quality.
primary_path.input: Token pair and accessible video URL.
primary_path.output: Asset response with preparing status, Asset ID, and Playback ID.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Mux begins downloading and transcoding; the original file is not stored in exact form.

primary_path.action: Wait until the asset's status becomes `ready`.
details: Webhooks are preferred; the guide permits low-volume polling of the asset endpoint no more than once per second.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Short videos often take seconds; large files or poor connections can take minutes or longer.

primary_path.action: Construct the playback URL from the returned Playback ID.
details: Do not substitute the Asset ID.
primary_path.output: Streaming URL and optional player or stream.new preview URL.
primary_path.success_signal: The generated URL contains the Playback ID.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Playback should not begin before ready state.

details: Mux describes this as a shareable automatically generated preview.
primary_path.success_signal: The submitted video streams in the preview.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An incorrect ID, non-ready asset, or private policy prevents playback.

branches.path: Install one of Mux's supported SDKs and submit the equivalent create-asset request with the same token and input URL.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The ready-state and browser playback signals remain the same.

branches.path: Configure a Mux webhook and wait for the asset-ready notification instead of polling.
branches.effect_on_first_success: A public callback and signature-handling setup replaces manual status checks.

friction_gates.description: A Token ID and Secret with Video Read and Write permissions must be generated and stored.
The input video must be accessible online and public playback must be requested for the shareable preview.
Mux must download, transcode, and package the asset before status becomes ready.

sources.title: Stream videos in five minutes
evidence_supported: Full credential, asset, wait, URL, and preview path

uncertainties.question: Does the five-minute claim include account creation?
uncertainties.impact: A brand-new user may take longer than the documented guide claim.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The page promises upload and playback in five minutes but begins from token generation in an account dashboard.

excluded_after_success: Captions, thumbnails, live streaming, and asset lifecycle management
